response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine systems-level depth (low-latency, high-throughput services using asynchronous I/O, fibers, and event-driven architectures), but the role's core requirement is hands-on modern C++ for industrial automation, and his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly six months ending in 2015 — C++ does not even appear in his skills list. He has no exposure to IEC 62443 / SDL practices, MQTT, OT protocols, or the industrial automation domain, and his last decade centers on TypeScript/Node web platforms. Strong engineer, wrong profile for this position.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-concurrency systems work (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching, event-driven design)
Broad full-stack and cross-platform track record spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Strong engineering-culture signals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, mentoring and workshop leadership
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months at National Instruments, 2014–2015); C++ absent from skills list
No industrial automation, IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or OT protocol experience
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node web development, with a career gap since October 2025

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / MQTT background
Strong systems and concurrency engineering fundamentals
